species: 'Candidatus Formimonas warabiya' (strain DCMF; dominant organism of anaerobic enrichment culture DFE)

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic defined bicarbonate-buffered mineral salts medium (composition per ref 24)

up.method: GC-FID depletion of methanol in culture DFE (batch, after 14-day lag); no depletion in abiotic control

sec.units: mol acetate per mol methanol consumed (±0.04)

yields.biomass_yield: 5.7 ± 1.4 x 10^14 DCMF cells per mole methanol utilised

growth_records.cite: Holland SI et al. Novel dichloromethane-fermenting bacteria in the Peptococcaceae family. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00881-y. [PMC8163858]
growth_records.snippet: Culture DFE consumed methanol at a rate of 309 ± 31 µM day-1 after a 14 day lag period, yielding 0.74 ± 0.04 mol acetate and 5.7 ± 1.4 × 10^14 DCMF cells per mole substrate utilised
growth_records.notes: Methanol volumetric uptake 309±31 uM/day and acetate yield 0.74±0.04 mol/mol methanol directly reported (Fig 2A). Methylotrophic acetogenesis via Wood-Ljungdahl pathway. No gDW biomass, so no mmol/gDW/h conversion. Temperature/pH not stated (cited ref 24). 14-day lag.

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic defined bicarbonate-buffered mineral salts medium (composition per ref 24)

up.method: LC-MS/MS depletion of choline in culture DFE (batch, after 11-day lag)

sec.units: mol acetate per mol choline consumed (±0.13)
mol MMA per mol choline consumed (±0.24)

yields.biomass_yield: 3.0 ± 0.9 x 10^14 DCMF cells per mole choline utilised

growth_records.cite: Holland SI et al. Novel dichloromethane-fermenting bacteria in the Peptococcaceae family. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00881-y. [PMC8163858]
growth_records.snippet: Culture DFE consumed choline at a rate of 344 ± 68 µM day-1 after an 11 day lag period, producing 3.1 ± 0.13 mol acetate and 1.3 ± 0.24 mol monomethylamine per mole choline utilised
growth_records.notes: Choline volumetric uptake 344±68 uM/day; product yields 3.1±0.13 mol acetate and 1.3±0.24 mol monomethylamine per mol choline directly reported (Fig 2B). Trimethylamine, dimethylamine, sarcosine, glycine not detected. 11-day lag. Temperature/pH not stated (cited ref 24).

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic defined bicarbonate-buffered mineral salts medium (composition per ref 24)

up.method: LC-MS/MS depletion of glycine betaine in culture DFE (batch, after 7-day lag)

sec.units: mol acetate per mol glycine betaine consumed (±0.08)
mol MMA per mol glycine betaine consumed (±0.13)

yields.biomass_yield: 1.1 ± 0.1 x 10^14 DCMF cells per mole glycine betaine utilised

growth_records.cite: Holland SI et al. Novel dichloromethane-fermenting bacteria in the Peptococcaceae family. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00881-y. [PMC8163858]
growth_records.snippet: The culture also consumed glycine betaine at a rate of 328 ± 26 µM day-1 after a 7 day lag period, producing 2.3 ± 0.08 mol acetate and 0.95 ± 0.13 mol monomethylamine per mole glycine betaine utilised
growth_records.notes: Glycine betaine volumetric uptake 328±26 uM/day; yields 2.3±0.08 mol acetate and 0.95±0.13 mol monomethylamine per mol directly reported (Fig 2C). 7-day lag. Temperature/pH not stated (cited ref 24).

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic defined bicarbonate-buffered mineral salts medium (composition per ref 24)

up.method: GC-PDD; 7.9±0.9 mM H2 consumed within 28 days

sec.method: GC-FID; no trimethylamine formed (glycine betaine not reductively cleaved to TMA)

yields.acetate_yield: ~3.4 mol acetate / mol glycine betaine (15 mM / 4.4 mM)
yields.monomethylamine_yield: ~1.25 mol MMA / mol glycine betaine (5.5 mM / 4.4 mM)

growth_records.cite: Holland SI et al. Novel dichloromethane-fermenting bacteria in the Peptococcaceae family. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00881-y. [PMC8163858]
growth_records.snippet: These cultures consumed all glycine betaine (4.4 ± 0.4 mM) and hydrogen (7.9 ± 0.9 mM) within 28 days, producing 15 ± 0.6 mM acetate and 5.5 ± 0.6 mM monomethylamine, but no trimethylamine
growth_records.notes: Endpoint concentrations reported (Fig 2D); volumetric rates CALCULATED by dividing consumed/produced amounts by 28-day duration: glycine betaine 4.4 mM/28 d = 157 uM/d; H2 7.9 mM/28 d = 282 uM/d; acetate 15 mM/28 d = 536 uM/d; MMA 5.5 mM/28 d = 196 uM/d. These are average endpoint rates (not initial slopes), so lower/less precise than the sole-substrate figures. Molar yields calculated: acetate 15/4.4=3.4; MMA 5.5/4.4=1.25. No TMA detected. Temperature/pH not stated (cited ref 24).
